Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
Donald Campbell chased a 300-mph record on Coniston Water in an ageing boat and tight finances. Narrated by Robert Powell.

The 1930s were the years of the depression and had a marked effect on Britain's railways. However the renewal of locomotives was urgent so there was considerable change in the steam locomotive fleets.
The F-15 Eagle immediately turned the tide of the first Gulf War to prove its greatness, and was involved in one of the most bizarre combat incidents during that conflict. S2 E6
An investigation into the systematic looting that took place in Paris during four years of Nazi occupation that tracks missing treasures to Berlin and Switzerland. Ep5: City of Loot
Some of the most shocking cases connected with the Nazi regime's bloody reign beginning with Hitler's niece Geli Raubal, who was found dead in his apartment in 1931. Ep1: Hitler's Niece
By September 1938, Hitler had occupied Austria and was intent on invading Czechoslovakia; the British and the French made it clear, though, that they would go to war against Germany if he did so. Ep2
A moving story about the Lancaster pilots who launched an attack on the Tirpitz; the men on both sides are so heroic and dignified, and we hear the story first-hand from these men.

programmes.show_name: Anzacs: In the Face of War
programmes.show_description: Archive footage of Australia and New Zealand forces during WW1, WW2, and the Vietnam conflict; this programme pays homage to those who so bravely sacrificed themselves.
David Beckham has had a football career that most players would be jealous of. In this 2009 retrospective we look back at his career.
The machine gun did not dominate the battlefield of WWII - unlike WWI - but it remained a highly potent weapon including being incorporated into the wings of fighter planes. Ep9: The Machine Gun
Four days before Christmas in 1988, Pan Am flight 103 fell in pieces from the night sky plummeting to the ground in the small Scottish town of Lockerbie where things would never be the same again.
The war dictated the need for new battle strategies and tactics, new weapons technology, and new medical practices to deal with obstacles and situations never previously experienced in warfare. E4
The B-24 Liberator more than proved its worth over Europe and the Pacific by patterning the B17 to deliver a blow to the Axis powers in WWII; however, it wasn't simply a bomb delivery system. S2 E2
Our look at mysteries that have baffled America continues with a look at the greatest unsolved art theft in history, the unexplained death of America's first mystery writer and if Bigfoot exists. E4
Bella turns the front of the house into a shop using Bill's compensation, Jessie gets engaged to Arthur, and Jack marries Dolly at a registry office. S1 E7: A First Time for Everything
Ray is assigned to look after a casino owner's wife, who has become a compulsive gambler. S8 E11: The Odds Couple
Ross resolves to reopen his family mine, Demelza struggles to fit in at Nampara, and Verity makes an unsuitable match that leads to a feud between Ross and Charles. Starring Aidan Turner. S1 E2
Having almost finished his report into Gene Hunt and his team, DCI Keats continues to push Alex to find out what really happened to Sam Tyler. S3 E7
Alf tries the patience of good natured Fred Johnson and his wife from next door by using their phone to make a very long-winded long distance call to Rita. S1 E4

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
Commander Buster Crabb vanished in 1956 while covertly inspecting a Soviet cruiser in Portsmouth. Was he taken prisoner or killed? Narrated by Robert Powell.
The Second World War virtually brought steam locomotive development to a halt in Great Britain - the watch-word became 'austerity' as engineers tried to produce simpler and cheaper designs.
The Hurricane will be remembered for the vital role it played in WWII, and how it destroyed more enemy aircraft during the Battle of Britain than all other air and ground defences combined. S3 E1
The team searches for Portrait of a Young Man, an irreplaceable painting by Raphael and the most valuable piece of art stolen by the Nazis during the Second World War. Ep6: Missing Masterpiece
The murder in 1943 of Sir Harry Oakes in the Bahamas, where the Duke of Windsor was governor at the time, saw him implicated in influencing the outcome of his murder trial. Ep2: The Duke of Windsor
Fresh from invading Czechoslovakia, Hitler now turned to his next target: Poland. Hitler saw this vast country as ripe for German expansion - and taking him to the borders of the Soviet Union. Ep3
World War Two would herald gigantic advances in military strategy. However, as far as the sea was concerned, the perils of the deep remained unchallenged: The Killer Wolf Packs.
...Bulge. Get a soldier's-eye view of the frozen front lines in the Ardennes, and learn how American troops thwarted the German's massive surprise offensive.
Fast patrol boats armed with torpedoes and machine guns were the marine raiders of World War II; this programme looks at some of their most spectacular raids. Ep10: Fast Attack Boats
...Where Were You? In June 1989, people took to the streets in China protesting for freedom - after weeks of peaceful protests, the government stepped in in an event that shocked the world.
From their early role in the War as observers we explore the increasing use of aircraft for bombing raids and combat and the development of aircraft and air corps on both sides of the conflict. E5

The B-25, often called 'the forgotten bomber', the mighty North American twin delivered the first blow in the fight back against Japan, in one of the most daring and audacious raids of all time. S2 E3

We continue counting down America's greatest unsolved mysteries including the infamous escape from Alcatraz, the hunt for the New Orleans axeman, and did Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid survive? E5
Mary has died when Jack meets old comrade Sid Hepburn, who is still in the Army; when Hepburn and a colleague take Jack to see Captain Leslie, he tries to recruit him. S1 E8: Paddy Boyle's Discharge
As Arthur attempts to ingratiate himself with members of the local golf club, DS Morley offers to sell him a police transport coach. S8 E12: The Coach That Came In From The Cold
Ross celebrates the opening of Wheal Leisure as rumours spread of a scandalous relationship between him and Demelza. Starring Aidan Turner. S1 E3
Tension mounts in the final episode of the police drama as Alex pursues her investigation to the bitter end and she and the rest of the team learn the truth about Gene Hunt. S3 E8
